def: s.m. Shyness, timorousness, timidity, &c. (see bhaṛkel).
s.f. = bharkā, q.v.
adj. Splendid, glittering, gorgeous, showy, flashy, gaudy; shy, skittish, coy; untamed, wild; timid.
adj. = bharmī, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Round, circular, spherical, globose; ambiguous, doubtful; confounded; scrupulous.

s.f. Roaming or wandering about; whirling round; wandering; giddiness, dizziness; the being in error or mistake, erroneousness; suspiciousness, suspicion, doubt; unsteadiness, vacillation; — worldly lust, sensual desire.

v.n. To be contained to fullness, be filled or filled up, be full; to be satisfied or sated; to be performed, be finished, be complete; to fill up, heal (as a wound); to be covered (as a mare), be lined (as a bitch); to be poured in; to abound, be replete (with, -se); to be loaded or laden, be charged (as a gun); to be stuffed, crammed, choked, stopped up (but in this sense bhar-jānā is the usual form); to be stored, stacked, heaped up, accumulated; to be watered or irrigated (as a field); to be steeped (in, -meṅ), soaked, drenched; to be painted, coloured, daubed, stained, polluted, defiled; to be cast (in a mould); to be paid in full (a debt), be refunded, be remitted; to be made good, be restored; to be full or ripe, come to a head, be full to bursting; to swell (as the feet, &c.); to develop, become plump or well-conditioned (the body); — v.t. To fill; to satisfy; to bestow in abundance, make rich; to perform, do, fulfil, complete, make good, discharge; to liquidate, pay, repay, re-imburse; to load, charge; to fill (colour, in -meṅ), to daub, paint, stain, defile, pollute; to soak or steep (in, -meṅ); to weave, plait, intertwine; to wind, twine; to give (evidence); to repeat (a creed, &c.); to pay (the penalty of), to undergo, suffer, bear, endure; to make (one) pay; to stir up, excite, instigate, incite
subs: v.n. To be or become full (as the heart, &c.); to be possessed or seized (by a spirit)
v.n. (intens. of, and = bharnā), To be filled, became full, &c. (see bharnā); to be broken-winded (a horse)
v.t. intens. of, and = bharnā, q.v.
v.t. To fill, to suffuse (used with āṅsū, e.g. āṅsū bhar-lānā, q.v.s.v. āṅsū)
v.t. To take the full amount due (to one), to exact a demand.

def: s.m. Act of filling, completing, &c.; the vessel that receives the expressed juice of the sugar-cane; debt; daily expenses.
s.m. contrac. of bharosā, q.v.
s.m. Hope, dependence, trust, reliance, assurance, confidence, faith (syn. āsrā)
subs: v.t. To give (one) hope; to reassure
(-kā, and -par), To hope (in), rely (on), trust (to), confide (in).

def: s.m. Load (of grass, hay, &c.).
s.m. Load (of grass, hay, &c.).
s.m. One who plies for hire.
s.m. One who plies for hire ( = bhaṛotī).
s.m. Stir, excitement, tumult; alarm, panic; incitement, stimulus
subs: (-meṅ), Tumult to arise; to be panic-striken
bharrā denā, v.t. To stir up, excite, incite, &c. = uksānā, q.v.

def: v.n. To become hoarse or husky.
s.m. Furnace, &c.; = bhāṛ; bhaṭṭī; qq.v.
s.f. Furnace, oven, &c. = bhāṛ, bhaṭ, qq.v.
s.f. Furnace, oven, &c. = bhāṛ, bhaṭ, qq.v.
s.m. A species of skylark ( = S. bharadvāja).
s.m. A mixed metal composed of copper and lead.
s.m. Cherisher, nourisher, supporter, protector; husband, lord
s.m. = bhatār, bhartā, qq.v.

s.f. Filling, insertion, stuffing, &c.; cargo, lading, &c. ( = bharat, q.v.); store, stock, accumulation; filling in, completion; admission, enrolment, enlistment, recruiting (soldiers); promotion (a vulg. use); — additional and irrelevant matter, invention, fabrication, falsehood
subs: v.t. To fill, load, lade; to store up, lay up, accumulate; to admit, enter (in a register), enrol, recruit, enlist; to engage or hire (a servant); to invent, fabricate
v.n. To be admitted, be enrolled, be enlisted.

def: s.m. A worker in the metal called bhart, a brazier.
s.m. (in comp.) = bhartā, 'Cherisher,' &c.
s.f. Upbraiding, reviling, cursing; reproach, curse, abuse; threat, menace.
s.m. Pimp; cuckold, &c. = bhaṛu,ā, q.u.
s.m. Name of Śiva; n. of Vishṇu; husband, lord; gold.

s.m. One who lives on the earnings of a prostitute; pimp, procurer, panderer; attendant on a dancing girl (who beats the mridang, and assists her in the chorus when she is singing); blackguard; fool, blockhead.
s.f. Panderism, pimping; earnings of a pimp.
s.m. A small species of jackal.
s.m. Fried meat.
s.f. = bharāʼī, q.v.
v.n. To pander, procure, pimp.
v.t. To cause to be filled, &c. = bharānā, q.v.
s.f. Wife, spouse; married woman; a musical mode; the wife of a rāg or deity presiding over a musical mode
subs: s.m. A husband under the rule of his wife, a hen-pecked husband.

def: adj. & s.m. To be borne; to be supported, to be cherished or nourished, to be maintained; dependent for a livelihood on another; — servant, dependent, mercenary.
s.m. Wifehood, state of being a wife; condition of a wife.
s.m. Ashes ( = bhasma, bhasam, q.v.)
s.f. Pudendum muliebre; mons veneris; the sun; a mouth.
s.m. = bhasakkū, q.v.
s.f. One who eats much, or often; stuffer, crammer, gormandizer.
v.t. To chew, to eat, eat up, devour, consume (see bhakshnā).
v.n. To fall, drop down.
v.t. To cause to float, to set afloat, to launch.
adj. Loose, flabby, flaccid; soft; weak ( = phasphasā; pilpilā).
s.f. Language, speech; the vernacular of any country; dialect; the Hindī language; a word; plaint at law; an epithet of Sarasvatī (the goddess of speech); one of the rāginīs or musical modes
subs: bhāshātmik (bhāshā + ātmaka) = bhāshā-bandh, adj. Written in a vernacular.

def: s.m. Speaking, talking, saying; speech, talk; language.
part. & s.m. Spoken, uttered, said; — speech, utterance.
s.m. Speaking, talking; any work in the common or vernacular speech; explanatory work, exposition, commentary.
s.f. A species of beet, Beta Bengalensis.
adj. & s.m. Light-causing, shining, glittering, resplendent; — the sun; fire; gold; a hero; name of a famous Hindū astronomer (commonly called Bhāskarāćārya); the plant Calotropis gigantea.
s.f. Ashes, cinders
subs: (S. bhasmasnān), s.m. The act of smearing the body with the ashes of cow-dung
s.m. A certain demon who had the power to reduce to ashes any one on whose head he placed his hand
s.f. The hemp plant; gāṅjhā (a cant term)
s.m. Meat burnt or roasted on cinders, chop, &c. = bhas-tikkā, q.v.
v.n. To rub ashes on the body
v.t. To reduce to ashes; to burn, calcine
s.f. The sissoo-tree, Dalbergia sissoo, or a variety of it
v.n. To be reduced to ashes; to be digested, consumed, utterly destroyed, &c.; to be overcome with rage, be in a great rage.

def: s.m. Morbid appetite with general decay; disease of the eyes, indistinctness of vision (arising from a thickening of the membranes)
s.m. What is reduced to ashes; ashes; anything utterly consumed or destroyed; any perishing thing.
s.f. Ashes; ashes of a corpse; — place of cremation
subs: s.m. The act of reducing to ashes; completely consuming or burning; calcining
part. Reduced to ashes calcined (as a metal).

def: v.n. To be manifest, to appear, be known.
v.n. To float, float away, be carried down the stream; to go down, sink; to be drowned. (In some of its meanings the word would seem to point to the S. rt. vah, as its source).
s.f. Bellows ( = bhaṭhī).
s.m. Husband's eldest brother, brother-in-law.
adj. & s.m. Shining, splendid; — crystal; a hero; Costus speciosus or Arabicus.
v.t. = bhasānā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Shining, radiant, &c. ( = bhāsur); — the sun, daylight; day; — Costus speciosus or Arabicus.

s.m. Advances to ploughmen without interest, &c. = bhātā and bhattā, qq.v.); — boiled rice; food; — a marriage ceremony which consists in the mother's family presenting rice, mūṅg, garments, money, &c. to the bride (generally on the second day's feast); — name of a soil to the north of the Ganges (that retains its humidity for a long time, and contains a large quantity of nitre); uneven ground
subs: s.m. The invitation to the grand-parents of the bride to attend the ceremony of bhāt and to make the usual presents (generally given by the bride's mother).

def: s.m. Dawn, early morning.
s.m. Name of a particular mixed caste of hereditary panegyrists; bard, minstrel, panegyrist, genealogist or family bard; eulogist; flatterer.
s.f. Fire-place, oven, furnace, kiln ( = bhaṭṭhā); pit; hole (especially of an animal, as a fox, &c.), cave, lair, den; — misfortune; curse
subs: To fall into misfortune, be unfortunate, be ruined or lost, to be cursed
intj. (colloq.) Go to blazes!; curse you!; hang it! rot it! (an exclamation of anger or disgust).

def: s.m. Warrior, hero; soldier, combatant; outcast of a particular tribe; barbarian.
s.m. A kind of grass.
s.m. = bhattā, q.v.
s.m. = bhāṭhā, q.v.
s.m. The egg-plant = bhanṭā, q.v.
s.m. A stone (used as a weight) = bāṭ, q.v.
s.f. The colocynth or bitter apple.
adj. Agreeable to the mind, pleasing, charming ( = bhāyā and man-bhāwan).
s.m. One who presents bhāt; maker of presents at the ceremony of bhāt; a relative of the bride or bridegroom on the mother's side; a term of abuse.
s.f. The avocation of a bhāṭ or bard; the encomiums of a bard, praise, panegyric, eulogy; adulation, flattery; — begging, mendicancy.
v.n. To go astray, to stray, wander, miss the right path, lose the path or way; to err, to deviate or depart from a right course; to be restless or disquieted, to long or pine (for, ke liye).
adj. (f. -ī), Wandering, straying, astray
subs: v.n. To go wandering about; to wander about being astray.

def: v.t. = bhaṭkānā, q.v.
s.f. A female of the bhāṭ caste; wife of a bhāṭ; female minstrel.
s.m. Husband
s.m. Name of a subdivision of the Gaur rājpūts.
s.f. Quiver (for arrows).
s.m. Flow, course; stream, current; tide, ebb-tide (see bhāṭhī).
s.f. Bellows = bhāṭhī, q.v.
s.f. Current, stream; down-stream; — adj. With the current ( = bhaṭhiyāl. — The word, in its Prakrit form, occurs in Sanskrit, in the compound rāya-bhāṭī, 'royal, or main, stream').
s.f. Bellows.
adj. = bhaṭhiyāl, q.v.
s.m. A kind of mars̤iya or elegy sung in praise of the brothers Hasan and Husain.
adj. With the current or stream, down the river and not with the flood tide.
v.n. To go down the river, or down stream; to ebb (the tide); — v.t. To cause to go down or sink; to ruin (a person).
s.m. One who keeps a sarāʼe, one who prepares victuals for travellers in a sarāʼe; innkeeper; sutler; baker.
s.f. Woman who carries on the business of an inn-keeper; wife of a bhaṭhiyārā.
s.f. Woman who carries on the business of an inn-keeper; wife of a bhaṭhiyārā.
s.m. Sarāʼe, caravansary, inn, eating-house; — low place.
s.m. The business, or the status, of a bhaṭhiyārā.
s.f. = bhāṅti, q.v.
s.f. Down-stream, &c. = bhāṭhī, q.v.
s.f. A Mohammadan ceremony which consists in sending cooked rice or other food, for three days, to the house in which a death has taken place; the food, &c. so sent.
s.m. Brother's son, nephew; wife's brother's son.
s.f. Brother's daughter, niece; wife's brother's daughter.
s.f. Wife of a bhāṭ, q.v.; woman or girl of the bhāṭ tribe ( = bhāṭan).
s.m. Land of the poorest kind (in the dialect of Sāgar and Bundelkhanḍ).
adj. = bhaṭhiyāl, q.v.

r: bhaṭiyār-pan, bhaṭeʼārpan, bhaṭiyārā, bhaṭeʼārā, bhaṭiyāran, bhaṭeʼārin, bhaṭiyārī, bhaṭeʼārī, bhaṭhiyārā, bhaṭhiyārpan, bhaṭhiyāran, bhaṭhiyārī
def: = H بهٿيارا भटियारा bhaṭiyārā, भतेआरा bhaṭeʼārā = H بهٿيارن भटियारन bhaṭiyāran भटेआरिन bhaṭeʼārin = H بهٿياري भटियारी bhaṭiyārī भटेआरी bhaṭeʼārī See the forms bhaṭhiyārā; bhaṭhiyārpan; bhaṭhiyāran; bhaṭhiyārī.
adj. Astray, wandering, lost.
v.t. To cause to stray or wander, to lead astray; to mislead, bewilder, baulk, deceive; to scare.
s.m. Name of a class of inferior Rājpūts.
s.f The prickly plant Solanum Jacquini (used medicinally for diarrhœa and coughs: — syn. bang-kaṭīyā; ban-kaṭaiyā).
s.f The prickly plant Solanum Jacquini (used medicinally for diarrhœa and coughs: — syn. bang-kaṭīyā; ban-kaṭaiyā).
s.m. = biṭmās, q.v.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Kāyaths.
s.m. Lands allotted to bhāṭs or bards; lands granted to Brāhmans.
s.m. Learned man, scholar; philosopher (esp. one conversant with the philosophical systems); a title of Maharatta Brahmans
subs: (bhaṭṭā+āćārya), s.m. A great doctor or philosopher; a title given to a learned Brāhman, or to any great teacher or celebrated instructor.

def: s.m. Advances to ploughmen without interest; ploughman's wages in kind; additional allowance, extra allowance or pay to public servants on special duty, or to officers and soldiers (commonly called 'batta'); subsistence money; travelling allowance
s.m. Cooked rice = bhāt, q.v.
s.m. = bhattā, q.v.
s.m. Bellows ( = bhāṭhī, q.v.); a yellow clay used as whitewash.
s.m. = bhātā; bhāṅṭā; and also = bhattā; qq.v.
adj. & s.m. Entitled to reverence, venerable; — learned man, doctor, philosopher, sage ( = bhaṭṭ).
s.m. = H بهٿهي भठ्ठी bhaṭṭhī, s.f. = bhaṭṭī, q.v.
s.f. = H بهٿها भट्ठा bhatthā, s.m. = bhaṭṭī, q.v.
s.f. = bhātī, q.v.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
s.f. Fire-place, grate or stove (as a goldsmith's, or a distiller's, or such as is used in an eastern bath); oven; forge, furnace, kiln; alembic, still; washerman's boiler or copper; distillery
subs: (among washermen), To turn the clothes into the copper, to boil the clothes
s.m. One who manufactures and sells spirituous liquors; distiller; one who keeps a liquor shop.

def: s.m. The country of the bhaṭṭī Rājpūts.
intj. O sister.
s.m. Bread or cakes made of mixed meal (chiefly that of arhar, ćanā, and mūṅg).
s.f. Light dry soil (yielding only an autumn crop).
s.m. Fear ( = bhay, q.v.).
s.m. = bhava, bhav, q.v.
s.f. (prov.) = bahū, q.v.
s.f. = bhauṅ; bhūmi, qq.v.
s.m. = bhāva, bhāʼo, q.v.
s.f. (irreg. pl. of bhau, or bhauṅ), Eyebrows.
adj. = bhaćak, bhićak, bhaćak, q.v.
s.f. Brother's wife ( = bhāwaj).
s.f. = bhaujāʼī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Pertaining to the earth, or the ground, terrestrial
adj. f. (see bhaum), Earthly, &c.; an epithet of Sītā.
s.f. The eyebrow
subs: bhaun ṭeṛhī karnā, bhauṅ ćaṛhānā, v.n. To raise the eyebrows, knit the brow, to frown, to scowl; to browbeat (syn. bhaweṅ tānnā, &c., q.v.s.v. bhaw, bhau).

ety: S. भ्रमणीयं, rt. भ्रम्; Prk. भमणिअं or भंवणिअं
def: v.n. To revolve, to whirl ( = bhamnā, bhāʼonā).
s.m. Large, strong post for chaining an elephant to.
adj. = bhay-ćak; bhićak or bhaćak, qq.v.
s.f. Eyebrows = bhauṅ, q.v.
s.f. Barking, bark (of a dog).
v.t. To cause (a dog) to bark.
s.m. A whirlpool, &c. = bhaṅwar, q.v.
s.m. Underground chamber, vault, cellar, cavern; cell, dungeon.

ety: S. भ्रमरकः; Prk. भमरओ; Ap. Prk. भवँरउ
def: s.m. Whirlpool, &c. ( = bhaṅwar, q.v.); a dilemma; a top; a large black bee (said to be enamoured of the lotus); a black beetle, dung-beetle.
adj. (f. -ī), Black as a bee (bhauṅrā), or as a beetle.
s.f. A bee ( = bhauṅrā); revolution, &c. ( = bhāṅwrī, q.v.); lock of hair or curl hanging down on the forehead, feathered hair; name of a defect in horses; — bread cooked or baked on embers.
v.n. To turn, whirl, revolve ( = bhamnā, bhāʼonā).
s.m. (prov.) = bhaṅwar, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī) = bhauṅrālā, q.v.
adj. Existing; made of matter; material; appertaining to the corporeal elements, elemental; appertaining to goblins or evil spirits
subs: s.f. Creation of beings, corporeal creation
s.m. College of monks, monastery, convent

def: s.f. Eyebrow ( = bhauṅ, q.v.)
subs: bhaweṅ teṛhī karnā, bhaweṅ ćaṛhānā, v.n. To raise the eyebrow, to knit the brow, to frown.

def: adj. = bhāyā, q.v.

s.m. Being, existence; birth; state or condition of being, natural state; nature, property, quality, disposition, temper; intention, design, purpose; signification, meaning; mind, heart, soul; emotion, sentiment, passion, affection, love, friendship, liking, choice, will; sudden idea or emotion of the mind, notion, idea; expression of amorous sentiment, or of one's meaning by signs, blandishment, gesticulation, acting, pantomime; — being, substance, thing, object; the world; superhuman power; — price, price current, value, rate
subs: (bhāva+artha), s.m. Simple or inherent meaning, obvious purport, drift, scope, sense, subject-matter
v.n. To exhibit or represent the passions (in singing and dancing), to gesticulate
(-kā), To raise the price or value (of)
(bhāva+ja), s.m. lit. 'Produced in the heart'; love
(-kā), To rise in price, be at a premium
bhāʼo ghaṭnā (-kā), To fall in price or value = bhāʼo utarnā
adj. Being in a state or condition.

def: s.m. Being, existing; existence, life; birth, origin; the place, or the means, of being; the world; time; acquisition, gain; prosperity, welfare; excellence; a god; an epithet of Śiva, and of Rudra
subs: (bhava+arnava), s.m. = bhavāmbu (bhava+ambu), bhavāmbu-rāsi, bhavāmbu-nāth, s.m. The ocean of life or of worldly existence, the world of being, the present life, the state of mortals
s.f. lit. 'Destroyer of existence'; an epithet of the goddess Kālī
s.m. The wheel or course of nature; the established order of created things
s.m. Concerns of the world, sublunary cares; the world's snares
s.f. lit. 'Night or darkness of existence'; moral blindness
bhau-sāgar, bhava-samudra, bhava-sindhu, s.m. The ocean of the world or existence, &c. ( = bhavāmbu, q.v.)
s.f. lit. 'Destroying worldly existence'; an epithet of the river Sarju (which flows from the Himalayas into the Ghāgra, in which it is lost).

def: s.f. = bhābhī; bhāwaj, qq.v.
s.m. Dancer; story-teller.

ety: S. भ्रातुर, gen. sing. of भ्रातृ+जाया
def: s.f. Brother's wife, sister-in-law; wife's brother's wife (syn. bhaujī; bhābhī).
s.m. See s.v. bhāva.
s.m. Friend, lover; — external expression of amatory sentiments.
s.m. See s.v. bhava.
s.m. Creator, producer, efficient cause; founder.
s.m. = bhāvnā, q.v.
s.m. Being, production, birth, nature; place of being or abiding, abode, habitation, dwelling, house, mansion; temple; site, spot; field; the various celestial paradises.
s.f. See s.v. bhava, bhav.
s.f. An epithet of the wife of Śiva, the goddess Pārvatī, Durgā, or Devī (in her mild and pacific form
subs: She is the patroness of ṭhags

def: Honorific or respectful pron. masc.; your honour, your highness, your worship, you (used respectfully); — his honour. his highness, &c.
s.m. Foot-pieces of a bed, &c.
s.m. See s.v. bhava.
s.m. See s.v. bhāva, bhāʼo.

= H بهاوين भावें bhāweṅ, postp. In the sentiment, perception, mind, notion or idea (of, -ke); in consideration (of); with regard (to); concerning, for; with a view (to), for the purpose (of), in order that; in the manner (of), after the fashion (of), like (as); in the eyes (of), near, before, to

= S بهاوي भावे bhāwe, postp. In the sentiment, perception, mind, notion or idea (of, -ke); in consideration (of); with regard (to); concerning, for; with a view (to), for the purpose (of), in order that; in the manner (of), after the fashion (of), like (as); in the eyes (of), near, before, to
s.m. Future time, the future
subs: adj. Dependent on, or subject to, the future.

def: adj. & s.m. Natural, innate; appertaining to feeling, sentimental; — (in Alg.) an equation involving products of unknown quantities.
s.f. Distinguished or handsome woman; lady, noble lady; a beloved woman; a wanton woman.
adj. & s.m. Future; what has yet to be, what will be, the future
subs: bhavishya-kāl, s.m. Time to come, futurity; (in Gram.) the future tense
bhavishya-kālīk, adj. Appertaining to future time, or to a future tense
bhavishyad-vaktā, bhavishyad-vādī, adj. & s.m. Speaking of the future, predicting, prophesying; — predicter, prophet
adj. Of or pertaining to the future
s.f. Voice of the future, prediction, prophecy.

def: adj. & s.m. Future; what has yet to be, what will be, the future
subs: bhavishya-kāl, s.m. Time to come, futurity; (in Gram.) the future tense
bhavishya-kālīk, adj. Appertaining to future time, or to a future tense
bhavishyad-vaktā, bhavishyad-vādī, adj. & s.m. Speaking of the future, predicting, prophesying; — predicter, prophet
adj. Of or pertaining to the future
s.f. Voice of the future, prediction, prophecy.

def: part. Animated, inspired; thoughtful, anxious, apprehensive, alarmed.
adj. Necessary or likely to be, or to occur, ordained by Heaven, decreed, fated, destined; unavoidable.
s.f. Necessity of coming into existence, or of taking place; fate, destiny.
adj. = bhavishyat, q.v.

s.m. Mental perception, present consciousness of past ideas or perceptions; recollection, imagination, fancy, presentiment; contemplation, thought, meditation, consideration; anxiety, apprehension, doubt, concern; wish. desire, craving, longing; solicitation, request; (in Math.) the principle of combination.
postp. = bhāweṅ, q.v.
s.m. = bhauṅrā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Future, about to be or to come to pass; possible, probable; — what will be, what must be; the possible or probable.
adj. & s.m. Likely or necessary to be or to occur; proper, fit, right, true; well-bred, polite; prosperous, happy; — existence, being; result, consequence, fruit.
s.f. = S بهويتو भव्यत्व bhavyatva, s.m. Good breeding, politeness, civility, courtesy.
s.m. = S بهويتا भव्यता bhavyatā, s.f. Good breeding, politeness, civility, courtesy.
s.m. Fear, apprehension, alarm, terror, dread (of, -se); danger
subs: bhay-ārta, bhay-āvishṭa, bhay-bhīta, bhay-mān, bhay-yukt, adj. Distressed or distracted with fear, frightened, alarmed, terrified; afraid, timid
s.m. Dispersion or dissipation of fear; — adj. Fear-destroying, fear-dispelling
bhay-darśak, bhay-darśī, adj. Imparting or occasioning fear, alarming, fearful, frightful, terrifying, formidable
bhay denā, v.t. To instil fear (into, -ko), to frighten, put in dread or terror, to terrify, scare; to intimidate, threaten, menace
s.m. Place of fear or of danger; seat of, or part exposed to, peril or hurt
adj. Causing fear, fearful, frightful, terrifying
part. adj. Shaken with fear, terrified
bhay khānā (-se), To be afraid (of), be in dread or terror (of)
v.n. To have fear, to be afraid, be apprehensive.

def: (Braj.), perf. part. & past tense, Came to be, became, took place, occurred ( = huʼā, from honā: fem. bhaʼī; pl. m. bhaʼe).
adj. (f. bhāʼī), Agreeable, pleasing, acceptable, fitting, suitable.
a dialect. form of bhayā ( = huʼā), q.v.
adj. Frightful, fearful, alarming, terrible, awful, dreadful; formidable; horrible; dangerous; dismal, solitary, lonesome, desolate.
adj. Fearful, horrible, terrible, terrific (see bhayānak).
adj. See s.v. bhay.
adj. Frightful, fearful, alarming, terrible, awful, dreadful; formidable; horrible; dangerous; dismal, solitary, lonesome, desolate.
adj. Frightful, fearful, alarming, terrible, awful, dreadful; formidable; horrible; dangerous; dismal, solitary, lonesome, desolate.
adj. Frightful, fearful, alarming, terrible, awful, dreadful; formidable; horrible; dangerous; dismal, solitary, lonesome, desolate.
adj. Astonished, astounded, confounded, amazed; frightened, alarmed, dismayed, terrified, awe-struck, aghast ( = bhićak, bhućak, bhauṅćakkā).
(Braj.) = bhayā, q.v.

s.m. Breaking, separation, disunion, difference, disagreement, interruption, disturbance; betrayal; breach, rupture, fracture; fissure, chasm, cleft; separation, difference, distinction, peculiarity; discrimination, discernment; kind, sort, species, variety; device; secrecy, secret, mystery; secret or hidden virtues or resources (of)
subs: v.n. To find out a secret, discover the secret (of), solve (a problem, riddle, &c.)
(-ko), To give a clue (to); to divulge or betray a secret
(-kā), To keep a secret; to keep one's own counsel
(-meṅ; -kā), To distinguish, discriminate; to classify, sort
(-kā), To divulge a secret, to betray; expose, unmask
(kā), To spy, pry into, get at the secret (of), worm ooneself into another's confidence, to sound.

def: adj. & s.m. Breaking, breaking up or open, dividing; causing dissension or disunion; breaker, interrupter; one who causes dissension or disunion, mischief-maker; spy, &c. ( = next, q.v.).
s.m. Scout, spy, secret agent ( = sandhānī); secret-keeper, confidant ( = bhediyā).
adj. & s.m. = bhedak; bhedakiyā, qq.v.; — discriminating, intelligent, knowing; — the ratan.
part. Divided, separated, severed.
s.m. Scout, &c. = bhedī; bhedakiyā, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. Having acquaintance (with), or knowledge (of); — one who is in the secret (of), secret-keeper, confidant; spy, &c. ( = bhedakiyā; bhediyā)
subs: (-se), To become acquainted (with); to be the confidant (of).

def: adj. Requiring to be separated or distinguished, separable; requiring to be kept secret, worthy of secrecy.
s.f. Proportionate share; instalment; rent of land, ground-rent
subs: s.m. A kind of complex tenure.

def: s.m. The brain
subs: (-kā), lit. 'To eat the brain (of)'; to annoy or tease exceedingly, to worry to death
s.f. The dorsal chamber of the head.

def: s.m. One who is sent, messenger, emissary, envoy

ety: bhej˚ = Prk. अब्भिज्ज(इ) = S. अभ्यज्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. अभि+अज्
def: v.t. To send, despatch, transmit; to bestow, grant, give; to utter, ejaculate
v.t. = bhijwānā, q.v.
s.m. = bhekh; bhes, q.v.
s.m. Frog, toad; a cloud.
s.m. = bhes, q.v.
s.f. Female frog; a small frog; the creeping plant Hydrocotyle asiatica.
s.f. Mixture, mixed or mingled state; that which is added and mixed, alloy
s.m. = bhilāwāṅ, q.v.
s.m. A ball or lump (especially of guṛ, squeezed tamarinds, &c.; see bhelī).
s.f. A pat, lump, or ball of guṛ or coarse sugar (generally four or five seers in weight).
s.m. A wry face.
s.f. The bleating of a sheep
s.m. The perennial water-plant Ӕschynomene aspera (Linn.), or Hedysarum lagenarium (Roxb.
s.f. = bhinḍī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Squint-eyed; — one who squints.
s.f. = H بهينٿا भेंटा bheṅṭā, s.m. = bheṭ, q.v.
s.m. = H بهينٿ भेंट bheṅṭ, s.f. = bheṭ, q.v.
v.n. = bheṭnā, q.v.
s.m. = bhed, q.v.
v.t. = bhigonā, q.v.
s.f. = bheṛ, bheri, q.v.
s.f. Sheep, ewe ( = bheṛī)
s.m. Ram; sheep (syn. meṅḍhā).
s.f. = bher, bheri, q.v.; — s.m. Bugler, piper.
s.f. = bher, bheri; bherī; qq.v.
s.f. Sheep, ewe.
s.f. A kind of musical instrument of copper, a kind of pipe or bugle; a kettle-drum.
s.f. She-wolf ( = bheṛnī, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Of or pertaining to sheep; like sheep; — sheep
subs: Following one another like sheep; blind imitation of another
s.m. Multitude crowded together like sheep in a pen; — following the example of another ( = bheṛiyā-ćāl).

def: s.m. Wolf.
v.t. To shut, close; to shut up; to close in, enclose, fence, pen; — to run by heats or spurts (syn. tag); to stop the mouth with a bribe, to bribe
s.f. She-wolf.
s.m. A quarrelsome or pugnacious man ( = bhiṛait, q.v.).
s.f. (Prov.) = bhaiṅs, q.v.

s.m. Appearance, look, aspect; resemblance, likeness, semblance; dress, guise, garb; colour, complexion; figure, form, shape; air, manner: feigned appearance, assumed likeness, counterfeit dress, disguise, personation, mask; any sect of Hindū jogīs or ascetics (syn. panth)
subs: bhes-banānā, bhes-bharnā, bhes-palaṭnā (apnā; -kā), To change (one's) appearance or garb, &c.; to disguise oneself; to put on a mask; to personate a character; to feign, simulate, counterfeit
adj. & s.m. Assuming an appearance, putting on a disguise; — disguised or hypocritical person.

def: s.m. Appearance, look, aspect; resemblance, likeness, semblance; dress, guise, garb; colour, complexion; figure, form, shape; air, manner: feigned appearance, assumed likeness, counterfeit dress, disguise, personation, mask; any sect of Hindū jogīs or ascetics (syn. panth)
subs: bhes-banānā, bhes-bharnā, bhes-palaṭnā (apnā; -kā), To change (one's) appearance or garb, &c.; to disguise oneself; to put on a mask; to personate a character; to feign, simulate, counterfeit
adj. & s.m. Assuming an appearance, putting on a disguise; — disguised or hypocritical person.

def: s.m. Medicament, drug, remedy; a species of fennel, Nigella indica.
s.f. Touch, contact; meeting, interview, visit; present (to a superior; syn. naẕr), offerings; sacrifice; hymn (in praise of a god or goddess); bribe, douceur
subs: s.m. Complimentary presentation of a goat to a superior; sacrifice of a goat
s.m. Deed of gift on the occasion of the bheṭ-bakrā
bheṭ denā, bheṭ karnā, v.t. To offer, present; to sacrifice
(-se), To meet (with), have an interview (with), to encounter; to be sacrificed, to fall a victim (to, -kī).

def: s.m. One who causes division, one who sows dissension; seditious or factious person; traitor.
s.m. The fruit of the egg-plant, the brinjal.
s.m. The act of meeting, &c. see bheṭ.
s.f. = bheṭū, q.v.
s.f. Present made to a superior on the occasion of an interview ( = bheṭ, q.v.).

ety: bheṭ˚ = Prk. अब्भट्टं(इ) = S. अभ्यट(ति), rt. अभि+अट्
def: v.t. To meet, come in contact (with), to embrace, to come across, encounter, come up to; to visit; to join company; to make a present to a superior (see bheṭ honā).
s.m. = bhetā, q.v.
s.m. Stalk, stem.
s.m. State or condition of being, innate property; nature, disposition, &c. ( = bhāv, q.v.).
s.m. (Poet.) = bheʼo, bhed, q.v.; — part. adj. = bhigoyā.
part. = bhewā, bhigoyā.
conj. Also, too, even, and, with; yet, still, besides, likewise, moreover, furthermore; — soever (in comp. e.g. kućh-bhī, Whatsoever, whatever
s.f. Squeezing, pressing; pressure, compression; narrowness; stinginess, close-fistedness, niggardliness
subs: (-meṅ), To practise niggardliness, &c. (in respect of), to grudge, stint; to hold back.

def: s.f. = bhikshā, q.v.
s.m. = bhikshuk, q.v.
To be broken or split in pieces, be bruised or crushed; to crumble to pieces; to shrivel, shrink, collapse; to be contracted or compressed; to be coerced, constrained or compelled.
v.t. To press, squeeze, compress, crush; to wring from, to force, compel, enforce; to grasp tightly; to stint, grudge; to hold back ( = bhīć karnā).
v.t. (Obs.) To penetrate; to sever, sunder, separate.

ety: perf. part. of bhīgnā, and = S. अभ्यक्त
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Wet, moist, saturated, soaked, steeped
subs: s.f. lit. 'Wet or soused cat'; cunning or crafty person (very quiet or subdued in appearance)
To make lame, improper, or absurd excuses.

def: v.t. To wet, moisten, soak, steep.
v.n. (prov.) = bhīgnā, q.v.

ety: bhīg˚ = Prk. अब्भिंगे(इ), or अब्भिंग(इ), fr. S. अभ्यङ्ग
def: v.n. To be wet, damp, or moist; to be passed in mirth and revelry (the night, rāt being expressed).
v.t. To wet soak, steep, &c. ( = bhigānā, bhigwānā)
subs: v.t. To make mock obeisance; to eulogize mockingly or in fun.

def: v.t. To cause to be soaked or drenched; to wet, soak, &c. ( = bhigānā).
s.m. Name of a tribe (supposed to be the aborigines of Ruhelkhanḍ).
part. adj. Moistened, wet, steeped, soaked; moist, damp.
v.t. To cause to send, &c. = bhijwānā, q.v.
v.t. To wet, &c. = bhigānā, q.v.

ety: bhīj˚ = Prk. अब्भिज्ज(इ) = S. अभ्यज्य(ते), pass. of rt. अभि+अञ्ज्
def: v.n. To be wet (i.q. bhīgṇā).

ety: bhīj˚ = Prk. भिज्ज(इ) = S. भिद्य(ते), pass. of rt. भिद्; — or Prk. अभिज्ज(इ) = S. अभ्यर्द्य(ते), pass. of rt. अभि+अर्द्
def: v.n. To be afflicted; to be affected (with grief).
v.t. To cause to send; to cause to be sent, to have (a person, or a thing) sent; to send
s.f. = bhīkh, q.v.
s.m. = bhikhārī, q.v.
s.m. = bhīkhārī, q.v.
s.f. Begging, mendicity; that which is obtained by begging, charity, alms
subs: s.m. Beggar's cup or vessel (in which he collects the scraps given in alms)
v.t. To cause (one) to solicit alms, or to beg.

def: = bhīkh: — bhikh-mangā = bhīkh-mangā.
s.m. Beggar, mendicant; — a proper name.
s.f. Unfilled or hollow grain; shrivelled grain.
s.f. Begging, asking alms; that which is obtained by begging, alms ( = bhīkh); begged food; — hire, service
subs: s.m. Beggar's bowl or wallet, any vessel in which alms are collected
(-se), To ask for alms (com. bhīkh māṅgnā)
s.m. One who receives alms, beggar, mendicant.

def: s.m. One who subsists on alms, beggar, mendicant, religious mendicant ( = bhikshuk; bhikhārī); the fourth Hindū āśram.
s.m. Beggar, mendicant ( = bhikshu).
